mnemonics.title: Three Main Integrity Constraints
mnemonics.content: ERD - Entity, Referential, Domain - Remember: Every Real Database needs Rules (E-R-D)

acronyms.usage: Unique identifier for each row, cannot be NULL
Column referencing PK in another table, enforces referential integrity
User-defined condition that values must satisfy
Column must contain a value, cannot be empty

visualTips.visualization: Think of PK as the ID card number - uniquely identifies you. FK is like your parent's contact in your school record - links to another table's PK.
Think of a tree: cutting the parent trunk (parent row) automatically falls all connected branches (child rows). SET NULL is like disconnecting the branch - it still exists but is detached (NULL).
Think of a classroom seat: (Row, Column) together identify each seat uniquely. Neither alone is sufficient.

analogies.analogy: Like a person's fingerprint - unique and always present. No two people have same fingerprint, and everyone has one.
Like a library book card system - you can't borrow if you don't have a valid library card (parent), and the book record (child) must reference an existing card.
Like a water bottle that can only hold liquid within its capacity - any overflow or empty bottle is not allowed.
CASCADE is like cutting both the parent plant and its seedlings; SET NULL is like removing the parent but leaving seedlings alive but orphaned.
